content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 4.5.1–4.5.3 WARN — App schedules automated hourly repeating push notifications (App/NotificationScheduler.swift:9-11, `repeats: true`, 3600s interval) and metadata explicitly advertises "EVERY HOUR... unlimited notifications" (fastlane/metadata/en-US/description.txt), matching Apple's spam-push pattern.
Pierre: Apple's 4.5.4/4.5.1 guidance explicitly forbids using push notifications for promotions/marketing sent on a fixed repeating schedule rather than in response to user action or discrete events; this looks like scheduled marketing spam rather than user-initiated alerts. Reviewers commonly reject or ask for clarification on such "every hour" promises. Recommend changing to event-driven, user-configurable alerts (e.g., user picks categories/frequency) and removing the "unlimited"/"every hour" marketing language from the description.
